rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring property definitions and constants are visible in the current scope before use in AGREE contracts.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers refer to valid AADL features or declared helpers with proper types.
Remove or correct AGREE guarantee statements that appear in implementation scopes instead of component type scopes.
generic_rules: When diagnostics report 'named thing must be an expression with a type', trace the first invalid identifier on the same line and resolve the root cause.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to avoid '<error>' type issues.
Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in valid component type scopes, not implementation scopes.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers or locally declared helpers; avoid using subcomponent ports directly in type contracts.
Ensure property definitions and constants are properly declared before being referenced in AGREE annexes.
Binary expressions in AGREE require both sides to be of the same type (int, real, or bool).
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; instead, correct references or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scopes; they are only allowed in component types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
Component type and implementation structure extraction
